messages.content: W badaniach starano się zatem „zrozumieć świat z perspektywy badanych, odczytać znaczenie ludzkich doświadczeń, odkryć świat ich życia uprzedni wobec naukowych wyjaśnień” (Kvale 2010: 19). - rephrase this

The research aimed to "understand the world from the perspective of the participants, interpret the significance of human experiences, and uncover their lived world prior to scientific explanations" (Kvale 2010: 19).
Nie wierzyli w to, dopiero jak te przykłady tych dzieci, jeden idzie do szkoły specjalnej, wypadają zęby tym dzieciom, bo wiadomo one najbliżej tego były. - rephrase this

They did not believe it until they saw examples of these children, one of them going to a special school, and the fact that these children were the closest to the situation is evident in their teeth falling out.

I will say that I noticed to the consciousness of people came very slowly. They did not believe it, only when these examples of these children, one goes to a special school, the teeth of these children fall out, because you know they were closest to it. these vegetables there ate directly from the garden, ate some uncooked, and in it 2% lead. It was who was interested in this, there was no such awareness, only later this awareness was [3_M_75]. - rephrase this

I observed that people gradually became aware of the situation. They didn't believe it until they encountered examples of children, one of whom attended a special school and experienced tooth loss, which indicated their proximity to the issue. These children consumed vegetables directly from the garden, some of which were eaten raw, containing 2% lead. Initially, there was no awareness or interest in this matter. It was only later that this awareness began to emerge. [3_M_75]

If the role of the metallurgical complex in the devastation of the environment was already noticed, at the same time the benefits that the community of Szopienice gained thanks to the operation of the steelworks were emphasized. - rephrase this

While the impact of the metallurgical complex on environmental devastation was acknowledged, equal attention was given to highlighting the benefits that the community of Szopienice derived from the operation of the steelworks.
In Silesia, a job well done was a testament to a person. - rephrase this
In Silesia, the quality of one's work was a testament to their skills and abilities.

Choć dla socjologów, a zwłaszcza tych, którzy zajmują się pamięcią jest jasne, że wiedza i oceny, w jakie wyposażony jest umysł jednostki oraz umiejętności wcielane w jej działania są zawsze zlokalizowane w określonym czasie historycznym, będąca zaś „produktem” socjalizacji jednostka jest zawsze człowiekiem tego czasu i miejsca, w którym żyje (Marody 2014: 23). - rephrase this

Although it is clear to sociologists, especially those studying memory, that knowledge and evaluations within an individual's mind, as well as the skills manifested in their actions, are always situated in a specific historical time, the individual, being a "product" of socialization, is always a person of that particular time and place in which they exist (Marody 2014: 23).

Although it is clear to sociologists, especially those studying memory, that knowledge and evaluations within an individual’s mind, as well as the skills manifested in their actions, are always situated in a specific historical time, the individual, being a “product” of socialization, is always a person of that particular time and place in which they exist (Marody 2014: 23). - devide this into few sentences

Sociologists, particularly those focusing on memory, understand that an individual's knowledge and evaluations, as well as the skills they exhibit, are inherently tied to a specific historical context. The individual, shaped by socialization, is inevitably a product of their time and place. This recognition emphasizes the influence of the surrounding environment on shaping one's beliefs and actions (Marody 2014: 23).

Although interest in it has gradually diminished, it has resurfaced in more and more discussions revolving around the nature of social reality. First with the functionalists, where it was associated with conformity, and later with the realization of the impact of accelerated social change and cultural transformation on the nature of everyday experience, the idea of normality was reevaluated so that it could reflect the changing context (Misztal 2016:104). - rephrase this

Despite a gradual decline in interest, the concept of normality has regained prominence in discussions concerning the nature of social reality. Initially linked to conformity within functionalist perspectives, its significance expanded as the recognition grew regarding the influence of rapid social change and cultural transformations on everyday experiences. As a result, the understanding of normality underwent reevaluation to better align with the evolving context (Misztal 2016:104).

As the researcher conducting studies on the issue of normality shows, important clues can be found both in the classics of the discipline (Auguste Comte, Emil Durkheim), as well as in later researchers (Erving Goffman, Harold Garfinkel) and those completely contemporary to us (Fiona Duncann, Mark Sladen). - rephrase this

According to the researcher conducting studies on the concept of normality, valuable insights can be gleaned from various sources. These include classical figures in the discipline such as Auguste Comte and Emil Durkheim, as well as more recent scholars like Erving Goffman, Harold Garfinkel, Fiona Duncann, and Mark Sladen, who provide contemporary perspectives on the subject.
For example, one can go back to Durkheim's classic work Principles of Sociological Method (2000) and use his understanding of normal social fact. - rephrase this

An example of drawing on classic literature is revisiting Durkheim's renowned publication, "Principles of Sociological Method" (2000), to gain insights into his conceptualization of normal social facts.

At that time, the situation of a metallurgical settlement appears to us in the context of life in socialist Poland, in which shifting environmental costs to the environment and limiting simple and extended reproduction of the workforce in favor of production was normal (see: Jałowiecki 2010). - rephrase this

Considering the context of life in socialist Poland, the condition of a metallurgical community at that time is understood as a situation where the environmental costs were shifted onto the environment and the workforce's basic and extended reproduction was constrained in favor of production. This state of affairs was considered normal during that era (see: Jałowiecki 2010).
